first_impression_why: A immediately reads as a product company with a tangible tool you can download, while B reads as a services/consulting firm with generic enterprise language.
reasoning: A wins because it communicates a specific, downloadable product with real traction metrics (26K+ downloads, 5.4K GitHub stars) — an investor can immediately understand the business. B has more visual polish and corporate logos, but reads as an IT services consultancy rather than a scalable tech startup, which is less compelling for seed-stage VC.
